summary: High‑energy set with scorching Dancin' and killer Estimated>Eyes jam
blurb: The first set maintains relentless momentum while the second set erupts into an explosive Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence and a fuzz‑laden Dancin' in the Street. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Phil’s bass drives the jams, and the drums provide nonstop power.
review: Portland’s Paramount Theater on October 1, 1977 delivered one of those late‑summer ’77 shows that still feels fresh. The band kicks off with a punchy first set—The Music Never Stopped, Passenger, and a tight Dire Wolf keep the groove rolling—then launches into a second set that’s pure fireworks. A standout Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World segue builds into a high‑octane climax, followed by a fuzz‑laden Dancin' In The Street that showcases Jerry’s new MXR distortion and Keith’s prominent keyboards. Black Peter, Not Fade Away and the rolling Around & Around round out the night with the kind of swing that makes this show a fan favorite. Across all available soundboard sources the Cantor capture (gd1977-10-01.sbd.cantor.gmb.86234.sbeok.flac16) offers the cleanest mix, balancing Jerry’s scorching lead and the thunderous drum work of Mickey and Billy while keeping Keith’s piano bright enough to cut through without overwhelming the band.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of five recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising the second‑set jams (Estimated>Eyes, Dancin'), Jerry’s guitar tone and the driving drum work. One mixed review notes a couple of cuts but still highlights strong playing, so sentiment remains solidly positive.

best_recording.reason: Clean Cantor soundboard capture with excellent guitar and drum clarity; minor keyboard mix issues don’t detract from the overall listening experience.

key_highlights: Explosive Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World segue
Fuzz‑laden Dancin' In The Street that puts Jerry on fire
Tight, driving drums and a four‑minute drum showcase
First‑set momentum with The Music Never Stopped and Passenger

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is scorching throughout—fuzz‑laden leads on Dancin', soaring solos on Eyes and tight riffs that drive the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass shines, especially in the Estimated Prophet → Eyes segment and his pre‑Dancin' solo; his bass coda fuels a dramatic drum break.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds solid rhythm guitar and strong vocal harmonies alongside Donna, anchoring the jams without stealing the spotlight.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboards are prominently mixed—sometimes a bit high—but add depth and drive, particularly on Estimated Prophet and the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are full‑on and intricate, providing relentless power that propels the set forward.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming pairs with Mickey for a four‑minute showcase that punctuates the second‑set energy.
